tests.intent: Relational monster, state, attack, drop, projectile, sound and pain definitions cover every reviewed E1M1 monster type without procedural type dispatch.
Independently authored states advance by relational next-state joins with exact durations, terminal corpse behavior, valid actions, and no client-selected transition.
Every spawn state reaches a terminal or recurrent legal component, every referenced next state exists, and no positive-tic state enters an unintended dead end.
Each of types 9, 58, 3001, 3002, and 3004 remains idle without stimulus and wakes exactly once with a stable target under reviewed perception.
Graph sound propagation traverses connected sectors in bounded breadth-first order, respects sound-blocking lines, handles cycles, and never uses geometric proximity alone.
Every reviewed monster wakes through an unblocked multi-sector sound path and remains idle when the only path crosses a sound-blocking boundary.
A set REJECT bit is an authoritative line-of-sight negative before intercept work while an unset bit is never incorrectly treated as positive visibility.
Exact rational intercept ordering blocks sight at the nearest one-sided or closed opening, permits open portals, handles endpoint ties stably, and rejects approximate sampling.
Every reviewed type acquires a visible player through exact intercepts and stays unawakened behind an earlier blocking intercept with stable event provenance.
Chase direction preference, turnaround avoidance, fallback order, collision probes, and identifier tie breaks are deterministic for axis, diagonal, and blocked choices.
Every reviewed type advances at its relational speed, cannot cross blocking geometry or solid actors, respects openings and radius, and repeats the same chase positions.
Each reviewed monster type has a focused in-range attack replay that applies its relational melee policy or deliberately selects its defined ranged attack without hidden dispatch.
Focused attacks for all five types prove only relational hitscan types emit ordered spread and damage reads, use exact intercept occlusion, and damage the stable nearest target.
Focused attacks for all five types prove only relational missile types spawn database-owned projectiles with stable owner, target, momentum, collision, and event ordinal.
Attack selection, spread, damage, pain and refire consume the reviewed database random table in fixed order with no reads during rejected or impossible attacks.
Every reviewed type enters or resists pain from its relational chance using the exact next random value, preserves target, advances timing, and never pains after death.
Lethal damage drives each type once through relational death states into a non-solid terminal corpse, emits one death event, and cannot attack or take repeat credit.
Every type receives a focused lethal replay; only relationally configured former-human types drop the exact pickup once at corpse coordinates and non-droppers create none.
Multiple actors advance from one immutable prior-tic snapshot in stable mobj order without one actor overwriting another state, target, random cursor, or event ordinal.
Idle, wake, chase, attack, pain, death, and drop replay for every reviewed type produces identical authoritative rows, events, random cursor, state hash, and frame hash.
Monster mutations are session-scoped inside the owning tic transaction, preserve other sessions, propagate faults, roll back atomically, and never commit autonomously.
Monster advancement consumes authoritative combat damage and projectile outcomes after reviewed world movement while preserving T7.1 inventory, ammo, player attacks, and event order.
Production uses relational joins and reviewed geometry while rejecting evaluator reads, fixture answers, hard-coded E1M1 identifiers, dynamic SQL, host randomness, and wall time.
Cyclic sound graphs, disconnected sectors, set REJECT bits, coincident intercepts, translated geometry, blocked diagonals, dead targets, and session decoys fail closed.
Every isolated semantic defect has an independent focused witness spanning state joins, sound, REJECT, intercepts, direction, attacks, pain, death, drops, ordering, and determinism.
